rationale: The thread closed #4891 as a duplicate of PR #4808 with pointer to #4809 for detail — Y mirrors that structure exactly (cites #4808 as the fix PR, #4809 as the tracking issue with more context) while X mislabels the duplicate target as #4809 and only mentions #4808 as a secondary fix source. X also asserts confidently and without hedge that the fix is 'already applied on main' and both PRs are merged, whereas Y specifically flags that #4808 shows as CLOSED/unmerged and asks for confirmation — appropriate epistemic humility given neither candidate has live access to upstream main. Y also surfaces a concrete, actionable gap (test coverage list missing this file) that ties the loose end together, making it more complete for a maintainer's actual next steps.
